title: Out-of-vault symlink content disclosed by walker read ops
description: Review finding (high, confirmed 3-vote): grep/links/doctor follow symlinks pointing outside the vault and disclose their content; ls/glob list them. Violates DESIGN §2 containment. Fix in the shared walker: skip symlinks whose canonical target escapes the vault root, and account for skipped entries.
notes:
FIX PLAN (P0):
Root cause: the shared walker (crates/wikid-core/src/vault.rs:46-54) already sets follow_links(false), which stops descent into symlinked DIRS — but symlinked FILES are still yielded as entries, and fs::read on them follows the target. So grep/links/doctor read out-of-vault content and ls/glob list the entries.
Steps:
1. In the walker filter (vault.rs), skip entries where file_type is a symlink (entry.path_is_symlink()); count them as skipped rather than silently dropping.
2. Defense in depth: before any walker-derived read, canonicalize and assert containment under vault root (the containment helper in paths.rs already exists for explicit targets — reuse it).
3. Decide + document in docs/DESIGN.md §2 what cat/edit on an explicit symlink path does (recommend: refuse with invalid_path or resolve-and-check-containment; must be consistent with the in-vault-symlink write bug in wikid-d8h low #6).
4. Tests in wikid-core: fixture with (a) symlink file -> /tmp target, (b) symlink dir -> /tmp dir, (c) in-vault symlink. Assert ls/glob don't list out-of-vault links, grep/doctor/links never read them, explicit cat errors per the documented rule.
Constraint: no new dependencies (DESIGN §9). Run full gauntlet before commit; never --no-verify.
close_reason: Fixed in c5ad7b3 via workflow fix phase: all 11 confirmed findings + lows applied, full gauntlet green, manual smoke test verified symlink containment, stale-days overflow, perms preservation, link resolution

title: skills cache trusts version label: content-fingerprint embedded skills (stale-skills bug)
description: A release or rebuild without a Cargo.toml version bump serves STALE materialized skills: the cache is keyed on crate version + a .complete marker that only asserts 'some materialization finished', not 'of these bytes'. Silently defeats the self-healing current-symlink machinery (heals only across version CHANGES). Live bug even after release automation: cargo-install-from-source, dev builds, CI all share one version across many commits.
design: Hash SKILLS bodies+references into the marker (or manifest.json beside .complete). materialize() (skills.rs:132) and status() (skills.rs:213) compare embedded vs materialized hash, re-materialize on drift. Add content_hash to SkillStatusResult --json. Taste-evaluator: highest priority; complementary to version bumps, not redundant. ~0.5 day.

title: cat: resolve #Heading/#^block fragments as promised by help text
description: taste-evaluator repro: 'wikid cat Alpha.md#Section One' and 'Alpha#Section One' return not_found although cat --help says wikilinks may carry fragments. Fix: strip/interpret the fragment — resolve the page part normally, and when a fragment is given, print only that heading's section (or the block line) like a wikilink dereference; error clearly if the fragment doesn't exist.
close_reason: verified hands-on; commit 0973fde

title: Obsidian compat: fragment-only wikilinks [[#Heading]] resolve to self
description: Obsidian treats [[#Child Section]] as a link to the containing file (appears in its own outgoing links, file self-listed). wikid currently drops fragment-only wikilinks entirely. Fix: emit them as outgoing links resolved to the containing page with the fragment kept; doctor must validate the heading/block exists in the same file (broken_heading_reference/broken_block_reference).
close_reason: Fixed in 36778c4; verified head-to-head vs Obsidian 1.12.7 and dike round-2 PASS (A/A/A/A/A)

title: Obsidian compat: file-relative and vault-absolute markdown link resolution
description: Head-to-head diff against real Obsidian 1.12.7 CLI showed: (1) [x](../Home.md) from Sub/Child.md must resolve file-relatively (Obsidian resolves to Home.md; wikid returns resolved=null and emits a false high-severity broken_links). (2) [x](./Sibling.md) from Sub/ must resolve file-relatively to Sub/Sibling.md (wikid wrongly resolves to root Sibling.md). (3) [x](/Sibling.md) vault-absolute must resolve from vault root (wikid drops the link entirely). (4) Bare paths like [a](Sibling.md) stay vault-wide with root winning — wikid already matches this. Rule: ./ and ../ prefixes are file-relative; / is vault-root; bare paths keep current link-path resolution.
close_reason: Fixed in 36778c4; verified head-to-head vs Obsidian 1.12.7 and dike round-2 PASS (A/A/A/A/A)

title: Fix fenced-code scanning and nested heading fragments
description: Unify fenced-code tracking across markdown scanners and validate nested Obsidian heading fragments by final segment. Requested final adversarial fix pass.
close_reason: Implemented shared fence tracker, nested heading fragment validation, tests, and validation gates.

title: Unify fenced-code scanners on fence_marker; fix nested heading fragments
description: Argus LAND-WITH-FIXES findings: (1) extract_atx_headings (doctor.rs), block_anchors (links.rs), extract_callouts (callouts.rs), and page_title (frontmatter.rs) use naive fence toggles that mis-scan documents mixing ``` and ~~~, causing proven false-positive broken_heading_reference/broken_block_reference; unify on tags.rs fence_marker delimiter tracking. (2) [[Note#H1#H2]] nested heading paths matched as literal 'H1#H2' -> false positive; match last segment.
close_reason: Fixed in b6147a0: shared FenceTracker (delimiter kind + min length) across all 5 scanners; nested heading fragments validate last segment. Verified against argus repro vault.

title: Ignore links in code spans and fences
description: Bug: extract_links currently reports wikilinks, embeds, and markdown links inside fenced code blocks and inline code spans, causing false doctor broken_links. Update extraction to skip ranges inside code while preserving other semantics.
close_reason: Fixed extract_links to skip fenced code blocks and inline code spans; added regression tests for backtick/tilde fences, inline code, unclosed fence, and post-fence extraction. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: extract_links must skip fenced code blocks and inline code spans
description: Found via post-polish smoke test: [[NotALink]] inside a ``` fence in a page is extracted as a link and flagged broken_links by doctor. Obsidian does not parse wikilinks/markdown links inside code fences or inline code spans. tags.rs and block_anchors already skip fences; links.rs does not. Both evaluators missed it.
close_reason: Fixed in 9fd4ac9: extract_links skips fences/inline code, verified on repro vault

title: Replace string-replace edit with hashline editing (cat --hashes + edit by line hash)
description: Product decision 2026-07-03: edit becomes hash-anchored, replacing string-replace. cat gains --hashes emitting line:hash: text (12-hex sha256 prefix per line, pantheon-pi hashline scheme). edit takes {line, expectedHash, newText} edits, refuses the whole batch if any hash is stale (structured error listing stale lines), atomic write. Rationale: stale-context detection under concurrent last-write-wins agents. Update SPEC.md §4 and DESIGN.md in the same change.
close_reason: hashline editing shipped: cat --hashes + edit --line/--hash/--new across core, HTTP, and CLI; docs updated

title: Add Karpathy-style LLM wiki demo vault

title: First-run UX: wikid serve auto-config + wikid init skeleton
description:
Two commands that make first contact frictionless. UX decisions locked with Ihor 2026-07-03.

== wikid serve (no config found anywhere in discovery chain) ==
1. Create ~/.config/wikid/config.toml and serve immediately in one shot (no 'edit and re-run' step).
2. Generated config: bind 127.0.0.1:7448; [wikis] registers cwd under its directory name (collision with existing name -> numeric suffix); [tokens] gets one generated random token named 'admin'.
3. SECURITY: never print the token value to stdout — serve output must say WHERE it is (e.g. "token 'admin' written to ~/.config/wikid/config.toml"), not what it is, so the secret can't leak into LLM transcripts when an agent runs serve. Write config with 0600 perms.
4. Token generation without new deps (DESIGN §9 is closed): read 32 bytes from /dev/urandom via std::fs, hex-encode. No rand crate.
5. Startup output (AXI): config path created, wikis served (name -> path), bind address, token location hint.

== wikid init [path] ==
1. Like git init: scaffolds cwd by default, or the given path (e.g. wikid init ~/wikid). Creates the Karpathy LLM-wiki skeleton (https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f):
   - index.md — content-oriented catalog hub: sections for entities/concepts/sources, each page listed with link + one-line summary; LLM updates it on every ingest
   - log.md — append-only chronological record with parseable entry prefix convention '## [YYYY-MM-DD] <ingest|query|lint> | <title>'
   - AGENTS.md — the schema layer: tells an LLM agent the three-layer architecture (raw/ immutable sources it never edits; wiki pages it owns; this schema), page conventions ([[wikilinks]], frontmatter), the ingest/query/lint workflows, index+log maintenance duties, and how to operate via wikid CLI commands (local + remote mode)
   - raw/ and raw/assets/ — immutable source drop + local images (Obsidian attachment folder convention)
2. Registers the wiki in ~/.config/wikid/config.toml ([wikis] name = dir name, absolute path), creating the config (incl. generated token, same rules as serve) if missing — init is the one-stop 'make this a wiki' command; wikid serve then serves everything ever init'ed.
3. Idempotent: never overwrite existing files — skip and report per file; registering an already-registered path is a no-op. Works on a non-empty dir (e.g. existing Obsidian vault: only adds what's missing).
4. AXI output: created/skipped per file, wiki name registered, next-step hint (wikid serve / wikid status). --json on both commands.

== Shared ==
- One config-writing/creation code path shared by serve and init (wikid-server config.rs or a new module; CLI calls it).
- docs/DESIGN.md must be updated in the same change: new init command in §6, serve auto-config behavior + config perms in §7, and the no-token-printing rule.
- Tests: serve-with-no-config creates file with 0600 + correct TOML + serves cwd under dir name; token value absent from stdout (assert!); init full + partial skeleton; init on existing vault adds only missing; collision suffix; e2e: init then serve then remote status.
notes:
ADDITION (Ihor, 2026-07-03): config-based default targeting for ALL commands.
Every command (ls, cat, grep, glob, write, edit, mv, rm, links, doctor, status, tree) must work with zero targeting flags by falling back to the configured wiki. Resolution order (first hit wins):
1. Explicit flags: --dir (local) / --server+--token+--wiki (remote). Both -> usage error (unchanged).
2. Env: $WIKID_DIR / $WIKID_SERVER+$WIKID_TOKEN+$WIKID_WIKI (unchanged).
3. NEW: config fallback from ~/.config/wikid/config.toml (same discovery chain as serve):
   a. cwd is inside a registered wiki's path -> LOCAL mode on that wiki (direct fs, no daemon needed).
   b. exactly one wiki registered -> that one (local mode via its path).
   c. multiple wikis -> optional default_wiki = "name" key in config; serve/init set it on FIRST registration only. If set -> use it; if unset -> structured error listing registered wiki names + hint: set default_wiki in <config path> or pass --wiki.
4. Nothing anywhere -> current structured error explaining --dir/--server (unchanged).
Notes: config fallback resolves to LOCAL mode via the wiki's path (tokens/daemon irrelevant for local fs access); remote stays opt-in via --server/env. Update DESIGN §6 targeting section in same change. Tests: each resolution tier + precedence overrides (flag beats env beats config), cwd-inside-wiki detection with nested dirs, multi-wiki error text lists names, default_wiki honored, 'wikid status' with no args after init just works.
close_reason: Implemented first-run UX: init scaffold/config registration, serve bootstrap, config fallback targeting, token show, docs, tests passing.

title: Apply adversarial-review fixes (11 confirmed + low-severity polish)
description: Fix all confirmed review findings (symlink escape via walker, grep totals, doctor overflow, atomic_write perms, unreadable-page handling, link title/percent-decoding, server force_required parity, remote mv e2e) plus justified low-severity polish, with regression tests.
close_reason: Fixed in c5ad7b3 via workflow fix phase: all 11 confirmed findings + lows applied, full gauntlet green, manual smoke test verified symlink containment, stale-days overflow, perms preservation, link resolution

title: Apply confirmed review findings (10 medium + 10 low)
description: Confirmed by 3-vote adversarial verification: HTTP DELETE-without-force error text differs from CLI/DESIGN §7; markdown link title syntax causes false broken_links; percent-encoded link targets never resolve (Obsidian invariant); doctor stale_days u64 multiply overflow panics on user input (CLI crash + dropped HTTP conn, two findings); write/edit reset perms to 0600 on overwrite; links returns wrong data when page unreadable; doctor silently excludes unreadable pages from checks and counts; remote-mode mv has no e2e/client test; grep totals conflate files-searched with files-matched + '1 matches' pluralization. Plus 10 unverified low-severity polish items held by the workflow fixer.
notes:
FIX PLAN — self-contained for a fresh session.
STEP 0: A workflow (wf_402431ff-624) was resuming the fix phase when this was written. First run: git -C ~/Projects/wikid log --oneline -5. If a 'fix: apply review findings' commit exists, verify + close instead of re-fixing. Baseline commit: 'feat: implement MVP' (all checks green).

CONFIRMED MEDIUMS (each needs a regression test):
M1 stale_days overflow — crates/wikid-core/src/doctor.rs:407 'stale_days * SECS_PER_DAY' panics (debug) / wraps (release) on huge --stale-days, reachable from CLI+HTTP. Use saturating_mul. Test with u64::MAX.
M2 perms reset on overwrite — crates/wikid-core/src/ops.rs:631 atomic_write: NamedTempFile is 0600; persist() clobbers existing file mode. If target exists, copy its mode onto the temp file before persist (unix cfg); else leave default. Test: chmod 755, overwrite via write+edit, assert mode preserved.
M3 markdown link title syntax — crates/wikid-core/src/links.rs: '[t](target "title")' keeps the title in the target -> false broken_links in doctor. Strip title portion when parsing. Test both quote styles.
M4 percent-encoded targets — links.rs: '[x](my%20note.md)' never resolves (Obsidian encodes spaces). Implement minimal %XX decoding inline (NO new deps — url crate is not in DESIGN §9). Test with spaces + unicode.
M5 links() on unreadable page returns Ok with wrong (empty outgoing) data — propagate the read error instead. Test with chmod 000 file.
M6 doctor silently excludes unreadable pages from all checks and page counts — surface them (e.g. scan_errors count in HealthReport or a dedicated issue entry, severity medium). Requires DESIGN §5 update in same change. Test.
M7 HTTP DELETE-without-force error text differs from CLI/DESIGN §7 — crates/wikid-server/src/app.rs:338-341 hand-rolls the message. Share one constructor with the CLI path (put it in wikid-core error.rs or match DESIGN's exact wording). Test asserts exact body.
M8 grep totals conflate searched-vs-matched + '1 matches' — crates/wikid/src/render.rs:100,125 prints total_matches/total_files where total_files = files SEARCHED. Add files_matched to GrepResult in core (serialized), render 'N matches in M files', fix singular/plural. Update DESIGN §3 if field is added. Fix json + human + tests.
M9 remote-mode mv untested — add mv (+ --force overwrite variant) to crates/wikid/tests/remote_mode.rs parity coverage.
(M10/M11 in the original list were duplicates of M1 and error-parity variants — 11 confirmed titles map onto M1-M9.)

LOWS (unverified, apply with judgment):
L1 grep/glob silently skip unreadable files — add skipped counter to results (ops.rs).
L2 = M2 (dup).
L3 core hints say 'pass force'/'pass all'; CLI flags are --force/--all — rephrase hints to work on both surfaces (error.rs:97,105).
L4 Vault::open on missing/file --dir gives generic not_found with useless 'run ls' hint — dedicated message: 'wiki directory not found: <path>', hint to check --dir/$WIKID_DIR (vault.rs).
L5 ls on explicitly-named gitignored file bypasses ignore rules while 'ls <dir>' hides it — route file-target early-return (ops.rs:205-221) through the ignore matcher.
L6 write/edit through in-vault symlink severs the link (rename lands on link, target keeps old content, silent divergence) — canonicalize destination before atomic_write when target exists. Coordinate with wikid-5vb decision.
L7 bearer-token compare not constant-time (app.rs:113) — manual constant-time fold over bytes (NO new deps; subtle/constant_time_eq not in §9).
L8 env-var remote targeting ($WIKID_SERVER/TOKEN/WIKI) has no happy-path test — add to remote_mode.rs.
L9 AXI item 7 trailing 'hint:' lines only tested for status — extend across ls/tree/cat/grep/glob/links/doctor (local_mode.rs).
L10 DESIGN self-contradiction: §2 says grep skips attachments, §3 + impl say grep includes UTF-8 text files — amend §2 to match §3/impl.

VERIFY + CLOSE: cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings && cargo build --workspace --all-targets && cargo test --workspace && RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --document-private-items. Commit 'fix: apply review findings (symlink containment, doctor overflow, perms, link parsing)' (hook re-runs gauntlet; never --no-verify). Then close wikid-5vb + wikid-d8h, close wikid-te2 (unblocks Obsidian/OKF/QMD/boxd tasks).
close_reason: Fixed in c5ad7b3 via workflow fix phase: all 11 confirmed findings + lows applied, full gauntlet green, manual smoke test verified symlink containment, stale-days overflow, perms preservation, link resolution

title: Deploy wikid to a boxd VM for real-world testing
description: Dogfood deployment: build a release binary (or build on the VM), provision a boxd VM, run 'wikid serve' with a config exposing a real test wiki (named wikis + bearer tokens), proxy it via the boxd HTTPS subdomain, and exercise it end-to-end from this machine in remote mode (--server/--token/--wiki) with a real agent workload. Validates: non-loopback token enforcement, config discovery, systemd-or-equivalent process supervision, and actual AXI ergonomics over the network. Use boxd-cli skill; consider boxd-setup-golden if it becomes a long-lived test env.
close_reason: Deployed wikid to boxd VM wikid-yws, configured systemd supervision, exposed HTTPS proxy on port 7448, verified auth enforcement, remote CLI read/write/edit/rm, and Pantheon read-only agent smoke workload.

title: Open Knowledge Format (OKF) support
description: Support OKF bundles (https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog/tree/main/okf) as first-class wikis: markdown + YAML frontmatter with a small set of required keys (type, resource, tags, timestamp), directory-hierarchy bundles with auto-generated index.md for progressive disclosure, and markdown-link concept graphs. wikid should: detect an OKF bundle, validate required frontmatter keys via doctor (new check severity: medium), surface frontmatter fields in ls/status/grep filters, and treat index.md hierarchy as the navigation root. Read the spec: SPEC.md in that repo. Blog: https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/how-the-open-knowledge-format-can-improve-data-sharing
notes: CORRECTION from spec research (thoughts/research/okf-compatibility.md, 2026-07-06): OKF SPEC.md v0.1 requires ONLY 'type' in frontmatter — resource/tags/timestamp are optional/recommended, and consumers MUST NOT reject on missing optional fields, unknown keys/types, broken links, or missing index.md (§4.2/§5.3/§9). Doctor check must exempt index.md/log.md at every level. Detection marker: okf_version in root index.md. Gaps: type-presence doctor check, frontmatter fields in ls/status/grep, okf_version detection, log.md scaffold format. Broken-link severity conflict (wikid High vs OKF tolerate) → suggested DoctorProfile::Okf. Doc lists 8 proposed sub-beads.

title: Pluggable search providers with QMD out of the box
description: Add a search-provider abstraction so wikid's built-in regex grep is one provider among several, and integrate QMD (https://github.com/tobi/qmd) out of the box: BM25 + vector + LLM-reranked hybrid search over markdown, runs locally, agent-friendly --json/--files output. Design: provider trait in wikid-core (or a sidecar model — QMD is a Node/Bun CLI, so likely shell-out with graceful absence detection), config per wiki (provider = builtin|qmd|...), CLI surface e.g. 'wikid search' (semantic/hybrid) distinct from 'wikid grep' (exact regex, stays dependency-free). Keep the no-LLM-inside invariant: QMD is an external tool wikid delegates to, never a bundled model.

title: Full Obsidian format support
description: Go beyond MVP wikilink basics to first-class Obsidian vault compatibility: embeds/transclusions (![[note]], ![[note#heading]]), tags (#tag inline + frontmatter tags/aliases), callouts, block references (^block-id), heading+alias link edge cases, and respecting .obsidian/ config where relevant (e.g. attachment folder). Doctor and links must understand these constructs (embeds count as links; aliases resolve). MVP already: [[link]]/[[link|alias]]/[[link#heading]], .obsidian/ ignored, YAML frontmatter parsing.
close_reason: Full Obsidian format support shipped across 24f084f..b6147a0: embeds+fragments, aliases, tags (CLI+HTTP), block/heading validation, callouts, .obsidian attachmentFolderPath. Validated by dike (PASS), taste-evaluator (9.25/10 pass), argus (LAND-WITH-FIXES, fixes landed), all hands-on against the built CLI. 303 tests green.

title: automate releases: release-please + release binary builds consumed by wikid update
description: Latest release v0.1.0 (Jul 3) while main is 37+ commits ahead; 'wikid update' reports 'up to date' on a stale binary — technically true, epistemically false. Conventional commits already enforced repo-wide, so release-please slots in directly.
design: release-please for version bump + CHANGELOG (the changelog IS part of the fix — honest public record of hidden commits). CI release job must emit assets named EXACTLY what update.rs greps for: wikid-<triple> + wikid-<triple>.sha256 for the four triples in target_triple() (update.rs:123-135); checksums generated in CI. Deliberate decision on the transient window where main's bumped version exceeds latest release (hits version_mismatch in resolve_plan, update.rs:153-165) — evaluator says honest, keep, but decide explicitly. Cut v0.2.0 as first automated release (skills feature).

title: embed build metadata in --version (git describe + content hash, structured --json)
description: Two very different binaries both print bare '0.1.0' (HEAD is 37 commits past the v0.1.0 tag). Makes staleness undiagnosable.
design: build.rs captures git describe into env var, wired into clap version (main.rs:34). ADDITIVE to semver — do not replace the string update/skills-keying depend on. Degrade gracefully in git-less builds (fallback CARGO_PKG_VERSION). Add structured 'wikid --version --json' {semver, git_describe, content_hash} for agents and the living-doc test. ~2-3h.

title: skills subcommand: bake agent-facing skills into the wikid binary (agent-browser pattern)
description:
Ihor reviewed how agent-browser (v0.29.0, homebrew) exposes skills and wants the same approach: skills baked into the CLI so they are always version-matched with the binary — no separate download, no copy-paste authoring pushed onto users.

## The agent-browser pattern (observed 2026-07-06)
- 'skills [list]' — one line per skill: name + truncated description (tier 1, ~10 lines).
- 'skills get core' — prints the skill's SKILL.md (~460 lines): frontmatter (name, description, allowed-tools) + workflow guide (tier 2).
- 'skills get core --full' — appends references/*.md and templates/*.sh (~2500 lines): full command reference, deep topics (tier 3).
- 'skills path [name]' — prints the on-disk skill directory so harnesses can symlink into ~/.claude/skills.
- Top of --help says: "Start here (for AI agents): agent-browser skills get core --full — Skills ship with the CLI (always version-matched)... Prefer this over guessing commands from flag docs alone."
- SKILL.md frontmatter is directly Claude-skill compatible, so 'get' output can be redirected into a skill file as-is.
- Layout: skill-data/<name>/{SKILL.md, references/*.md, templates/*.sh}.

## wikid adaptation
- Embed skill sources at compile time (include_str!/include_dir from a top-level skills/ dir) — single static binary stays the product; skills can never drift from the binary version.
- 'wikid skills' / 'wikid skills get core [--full]' print to stdout; --json wraps as usual.
- 'wikid skills path' on a pure static binary: materialize embedded files into a per-version cache dir (e.g. ~/.local/share/wikid/skills/<version>/) and print it, so symlink-into-.claude/skills works; re-materialize on version change. Alternatively/additionally 'wikid skills install --claude-code [dir]' writes into .claude/skills directly.
- Update wikid --help header with the 'Start here (for AI agents): wikid skills get core' pointer (replaces the README 'paste this paragraph into CLAUDE.md' guidance; README section rewritten to point at the skills command).
- 'wikid init' can drop a pointer into the generated AGENTS.md rather than duplicating skill content.

## Skill content (initial set)
- core: the surface + workflows — grep-before-write, cat --hashes → edit/edit-batch optimistic-concurrency protocol, cat fragments (#Heading / #^block-id), wikilinks + resolution semantics (root-wins, ./ ../ / rules, alias extension), tags incl. implied ancestors, doctor after bulk edits, remote-mode env vars (WIKID_SERVER/TOKEN/WIKI), structured errors + hint lines.
- llm-wiki: conventions for Karpathy-style agent wikis — index.md/log.md discipline, SCHEMA.md frontmatter, raw/ vs authored pages, when to split pages (matches init scaffold and doctor's llm-wiki profile).
- references/ (behind --full): JSON output shapes per command, doctor check catalog with severities, link-resolution precedence table.

## Progressive disclosure tiers
frontmatter description (always-in-context sentence) → SKILL.md body (loaded on demand) → --full references. Keep tier 2 under ~500 lines like agent-browser's core.

## Acceptance
- skills list/get/get --full/path work in local AND remote mode (content is client-side, no server round-trip).
- Output of 'skills get core' is a valid Claude Code skill file (frontmatter intact).
- --help header carries the start-here pointer.
- README 'Giving your agents the wiki' section rewritten around it.
- DESIGN.md updated with the subcommand + embedding approach (design-authority rule).
notes: Shipped across 5 rounds: 8515aa0 (feature), 0ce5b35/ffa2b32/15cd119 (evaluator fixes incl. doctor scope + init --dir bugs), ebaaf4c (column grid, self-healing current-based skills path, skills status installation linter, living-doc CI test), a60bb24 (status rendering polish). Validated: comprehension eval (agents reproduced workflows from skills alone), Anthropic best-practices audit 13/14→14/14, taste-evaluator rounds 7.9→6.5→8.1→8.9→9.2 PASS (D9/O9/C9/F10, zero caps).
close_reason: Complete: taste-evaluator PASS at 9.2, all agent validations green

title: Add 'wikid tags related <tag>' — co-occurrence relatedness via PMI
description:
Agents can see raw tag counts (wikid tags) but have no way to learn which tags are related IN THIS VAULT — vault-specific relatedness is knowledge no LLM prior contains, while general semantic similarity is something agents already have for free. Expose structural relatedness so agents can connect ideas.

Scope:
- 'wikid tags related <tag>': rank other tags by pointwise mutual information (PMI) over shared pages, computed from tag-page co-occurrence.
- Reuses the shared vault scan from wikid-688 (pages + tags already extracted by tags.rs).
- Pure Rust, deterministic, offline — no embeddings, no models, no network (semantic search is deliberately delegated to companion tools like qmd, see wikid qmd-synergy bead).
- Output follows render.rs house style: count header, take(N) truncation with '… N more; use --json', trailing hint line. JSON via global --json flag: [{tag, pmi, shared_pages, pages: [...take limited]}].
- Guard low-signal cases: tags with 1 occurrence produce degenerate PMI — apply a min-count floor and say so in output.
design: PMI over co-occurrence, not embeddings: the agent IS the semantic engine; wikid's job is to surface structural facts the agent cannot cheaply compute. Keeps wikid dependency-light and deterministic.
acceptance_criteria: wikid tags related <tag> ranks co-occurring tags by PMI; deterministic output; min-count floor documented; --json emits full ranked list; human output matches render.rs conventions

title: edit: add --before/--after insertion (and consider append) instead of replace-line tricks
description:
AX finding from real dogfooding (2026-07-06, migrating research docs into projects-wiki via wikid): there is no insertion primitive, only line replacement. To prepend a log entry to log.md (newest-first convention) the agent had to run: wikid edit log.md --line 7 --hash <hash> --new=$'## [date] new entry\n\n- body\n\n<original line 7 text>' — i.e. replace an existing line with itself plus the new lines. It works but is error-prone (must re-quote the original line verbatim) and obscures intent.

Proposal: extend edit with mutually-exclusive --before / --after flags that insert --new text at the hash-guarded line instead of replacing it:
  wikid edit log.md --line 7 --hash 79f8f364db48 --before --new='## [2026-07-06] ...'
The existing --line/--hash optimistic-concurrency guard stays exactly as-is (refuse if the anchor line changed since read) — that guard is the best part of the current design and is why insertion should anchor to a hashed line rather than a bare line number. Multiline --new already expands to several lines; keep that.

Also consider a plain 'append <path>' verb (stdin or -m) for log-style pages where anchoring to EOF needs no hash guard.

Scope: CLI + HTTP server + MCP surface + edit-batch (batch entries could take an optional position field: replace|before|after, default replace, additive JSON). Update cat --hashes docs/help. Regression tests: insert before/after first/last line, hash mismatch refusal, multiline insertion, edit-batch mixed positions.

Why it matters: wikid's target users are agents; log.md/index.md conventions (prepend newest, insert into a section) are the single most common write pattern in real vaults, and today each such edit risks corrupting the anchor line.

title: Obsidian compat: markdown pure-anchor links [text](#Heading) should self-resolve
description: dike finding (low, pre-existing): [jump](#Heading One) markdown links are dropped from outgoing links, while [[#Heading One]] self-resolves after wikid-4zw. Real Obsidian treats both as intra-file links. Fix: parse #-only markdown link targets as self-links with fragment kept; doctor validates fragment against the containing page like fragment-only wikilinks. Repro: page.md with '# Heading One' + '[jump](#Heading One)' -> links --json omits the markdown anchor.
notes: Implemented markdown pure-anchor self-resolution in commit 50b291a; validation passed: cargo fmt --all --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace. Bead intentionally left open per user request.
close_reason: Fixed in 50b291a: [text](#Heading) markdown anchors self-resolve with fragment, doctor validates heading/block anchors; verified hands-on, gate green (321 tests)

title: Add wikid graph command exposing the vault link graph
description:
Agents (and humans) have no way to see the vault's link graph as a whole — links <path> is page-scoped and doctor only surfaces issues, even though wikid-core already computes full-vault link extraction and resolution on every links/doctor call. Expose it as a new 'wikid graph' command so agents can analyze/render the graph however they want.

Scope (revised after taste-evaluator review, verdict 5/10 revise):
- wikid-core: extract a shared vault scan (pages + extracted links + LinkIndex) reused by links(), doctor(), and new graph() — today the sweep is duplicated.
- 'wikid graph' default output is a SUMMARY, not a dump (consistent with cat/doctor truncation philosophy): node/edge counts, resolved/ambiguous/broken tallies, orphan count, degree distribution, top-K hubs. Full edge list is opt-in via --full/--edges.
- JSON schema: every link is an edge with status — {from, to|null, status: resolved|ambiguous|broken, candidates: [...], kind, embed, count}. No separate unresolved array, no ghost nodes. Preserve the resolver's three-state Resolution enum. Dedup edges keyed on (from, to, kind, embed) with count.
- 'wikid graph <path> --depth N' — local neighborhood BFS, default --direction both (also out/in). Document that depth-1 both is a superset of 'links <path>'.
- --include/--exclude glob filtering to scope to a subtree.
- Human output follows render.rs house style: count headers, arrows, take(N) truncation with '… N more; use --json', trailing hint: line.

Deferred (cut from v1 as Obsidian cargo-cult): --format dot|mermaid, tags-as-nodes (if ever added, nodes need type: file|tag discriminator).
design: Separate 'graph' subcommand (not an extension of 'links') — links stays the page-scoped report, graph is the vault-scoped machine substrate. Summary-first default is non-negotiable: a 2000-page vault edge dump would blow an agent context window. Keep Ambiguous as a first-class edge status since links.rs Resolution already models it.
acceptance_criteria: wikid graph prints summary stats; wikid graph --full emits deduped status-tagged edges as JSON with --json; wikid graph <path> --depth N works with --direction both/out/in; include/exclude globs scope the scan; links()/doctor()/graph() share one vault scan; human output matches render.rs conventions

title: tags --json: mark implied ancestor tags with implied:true
description: taste-evaluator: after nested-tag ancestor expansion (wikid-gx7), agents cannot tell literal tags from implied ancestors in tags --json. Add additive boolean field (implied: true) on ancestor entries that never occur literally; omit for literal tags to keep wire compat.
close_reason: verified hands-on against fixture vault; commit 0973fde

title: Obsidian compat: tags parity — implied parent tags and occurrence counts
description: Obsidian 'tags counts' on fixture: #project:1 (implied parent of #project/wikid, no literal occurrence), #work:3 (occurrences: Home inline + Beta frontmatter + Beta inline). wikid emits project/wikid only (no parent) and work:2 (count=page count, not occurrences). Fix: count = total occurrences (frontmatter entries count once each, inline each occurrence); expand nested tags into implied parents whose count aggregates all descendant occurrences; keep the pages array (union for parents).
close_reason: Fixed in 36778c4; verified head-to-head vs Obsidian 1.12.7 and dike round-2 PASS (A/A/A/A/A)

title: Polish Obsidian support evaluator findings
description: Apply five small evaluator fixes for Obsidian format support: escaped hashes in tags, CLI not-found extension hints, concise help text updates, sanitized malformed frontmatter doctor detail, and proactive duplicate alias doctor finding. Validate with workspace tests/clippy/fmt, commit, and push.
close_reason: Implemented five Obsidian polish fixes with tests; cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, and cargo test --workspace pass.

title: Polish fixes from dike + taste-evaluator review of Obsidian support
description: Evaluators passed the feature (dike PASS, taste 9.25/10) but surfaced polish items: escaped \# still extracted as tag; extensionless cat/read gives bare not_found without .md hint; CLI help omits #heading/#^block fragment semantics; malformed_frontmatter leaks raw serde_yaml errors; duplicate aliases only flagged when linked (should be proactive low-severity).
close_reason: All 5 polish fixes landed in b8e1036, verified by gate + hands-on smoke test (hint, duplicate_aliases, escaped hash, sanitized YAML, help text)

title: Implement Phase 1 Obsidian embed link graph support
description: Add additive embed and fragment fields to Link/ExtractedLink, preserve resolution semantics, mark embeds in human links output, and add Phase 1 tests from docs/tasks/full-obsidian-format-support.md.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace passes; cargo clippy --workspace passes; cargo fmt --check passes
notes: Implemented Phase 1 embed/fragment link graph support. Validation: cargo test --workspace; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --workspace; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings.
close_reason: Phase 1 implemented and validation passed

title: Add wikid update self-updater
description: Add wikid update command for explicit self-update from GitHub release assets, with checksum verification, docs, and tests.
notes: Implemented wikid update self-updater; release workflow now publishes raw binary + sha256 assets; health exposes version; reconciled concurrent doctor-profile/link-parser drift to keep validation green. Validation: cargo fmt --all; cargo test; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --document-private-items.
close_reason: Implemented wikid update self-updater with tests/docs and passing validation.

title: Triage agent feedback on wikid UX gaps
notes: Taste-evaluator implementation grading complete: PASS 10/10. Rubric: clarity/safety/agent comprehensibility/friction reduction/AXI fit all 10/10; no required fixes. Local validation passed: cargo fmt --all; cargo test; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings; RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --document-private-items; cargo build. Session: wikid-qig-taste-implementation-grade
close_reason: Implemented wikid CLI/doctor UX improvements with tests, docs, validation, and taste-evaluator PASS 10/10.

title: Make AGENTS.md canonical agent doc, remove CLAUDE.md
description: AGENTS.md contains only beads boilerplate with zero project context; CLAUDE.md holds all real guidance. Non-Claude agents read AGENTS.md and learn nothing. Move project guidance into AGENTS.md, drop duplicated beads quick-ref, delete CLAUDE.md. Also clarify README quickstart that install.sh compiles from source.
close_reason: Closed

title: install.sh: prefer prebuilt release binaries over compiling
description: Release workflow now publishes tarballs per target (wikid-<target>.tar.gz + sha256) on v* tags. Update install.sh to detect OS/arch, download and verify the latest release binary, and only fall back to rustup+cargo build when no binary matches. Cuts install from minutes to seconds.

title: README/docs: comparison table vs alternatives
description: Marketability: people arrive asking 'why not just X'. Add a section comparing wikid vs obsidian MCP plugins, git-based sync, Notion/database tools, plain SSH+files. Differentiators: no plugin, headless, one binary, no state, agent-optimized (AXI) output.

title: Launch posts: Show HN, r/ObsidianMD, r/LocalLLaMA, r/ClaudeAI
description: Marketability: write launch posts tuned per community. Obsidian angle: your vault stays plain files, zero lock-in, no plugin, works headless. Agent angle: shared memory/knowledge base for agents across machines, Karpathy LLM-wiki as a service. Post after demo GIF and v0.1.0 release exist.

title: README demo: record terminal GIF/asciinema of serve + remote grep/edit
description: Marketability: a 20-second demo at the top of the README does more than prose. Record wikid serve on one machine and wikid grep/cat/edit from another (asciinema or vhs), showing the file changing in the vault. Embed at the top of README.

title: Add one-line wikid installer
close_reason: Done: added public GitHub raw one-line installer, bootstraps Rust when cargo is missing, and proved it on disposable boxd VM wikid-install-proof.

title: surface client/server version+protocol skew in remote mode
description: wikid-server /health already returns version (app.rs:90) but the remote client (remote.rs) discards it — skew is invisible. Skew is a compatibility question, not a cosmetic nag.
design: Add a protocol/API version distinct from release semver to /health; client compares in the existing status() call path in remote.rs; emit warning[server_skew]: + hint on stderr (never free text in --json stdout — structured field if agents need it). Naturally CI-safe (remote mode only). KILLED by taste-evaluator: ambient gh-style background update notifier (network in agent loops, air-gapped CI failures, telemetry surprise) — remote freshness stays on explicit 'wikid update [--check]' only. ~0.5 day.

title: Evaluate qmd/rqmd as wikid's semantic layer — companion, embedded, or paid cloud feature
description:
qmd (https://github.com/tobi/qmd) is a local, offline hybrid search engine for markdown: SQLite FTS5 BM25 + sqlite-vec embeddings (EmbeddingGemma 300M) + Qwen3 reranker/query-expansion, JSON output, MCP server (stdio+HTTP), TypeScript SDK. It is exactly the 'semantic layer v2' deliberately kept OUT of wikid — validating wikid's structural-only, dependency-light scope.

What to do:
- Install qmd, index the projects wiki vault, evaluate search quality on real queries (vsearch + hybrid query mode).
- Check indexing coexistence: qmd index location vs vault (must not pollute the vault or trip wikid doctor; likely lives outside or needs ignore rules).
- Define and document the combined agent workflow: wikid graph/links/tags = structure + writes + health; qmd search/vsearch/query = semantic retrieval; bridges (see bridge-detection bead) proposes candidates, qmd vsearch confirms semantic proximity.
- Decide integration depth: (a) documentation-only — recommend qmd alongside wikid in the wiki skill / AGENTS docs; (b) 'wikid doctor' or init hint when qmd index present; (c) deeper (wikid shells out to qmd) — likely rejected, keep tools composable Unix-style.
- Outcome: a short written recommendation + updates to the obsidian/wiki skill docs if adopted.

title: Add bridge/gap detection — surface unlinked but topically-close vault regions
description:
The serendipity primitive: find pairs of pages/clusters that are structurally distant (no links, no shared tags, far apart in the link graph) but topically close (shared salient vocabulary via TF-IDF-style term overlap). These are candidate 'missing connections' — two islands of the wiki talking about the same thing without knowing it. Agents consume the candidate pairs and apply their own semantic judgment to decide which bridges are actually interesting new-idea material.

Scope:
- 'wikid bridges' (name TBD): emit top-K candidate pairs {page_a, page_b, shared_terms: [...], graph_distance, score}.
- Structural distance from the wikid-688 graph adjacency; topical closeness from deterministic TF-IDF term overlap over page content — no embeddings, no models, offline.
- Summary-first output per house style; full list behind --json.
- Optional synergy (document, don't depend): an agent can intersect these candidates with qmd vsearch results for embedding-grade semantic confirmation — wikid proposes structurally, qmd/the agent disposes semantically.
design: Division of labor: wikid surfaces where structure is MISSING (cheap, deterministic, vault-specific); semantic ranking of the candidates is left to the agent or companion tools (qmd). TF-IDF overlap is good enough for candidate generation — precision comes from the consumer, not the CLI.
acceptance_criteria: command emits deterministic ranked candidate pairs with shared terms and graph distance; excludes already-linked pairs and same-directory trivial pairs; summary-first human output; --json full detail
